label: Transfusion (procedure)
why: Paragraph says transfusion without specifying red blood cell transfusion; generic transfusion procedure is clinically acceptable.

label: Blood Transfusion
why: Paragraph says transfusion without specifying red blood cell transfusion; blood transfusion is clinically acceptable.

label: Exacerbation of asthma
why: For asthma exacerbation paragraphs, the exacerbation concept is more specific and should satisfy the base asthma target.

label: Rhinovirus infection
why: Paragraph says asthma exacerbation triggered by rhinovirus; rhinovirus infection is clinically acceptable for the organism/trigger target.

label: Human rhinovirus
why: Human rhinovirus is clinically acceptable for the rhinovirus target.

label: Epinephrine
why: The paragraph says nebulized epinephrine; base epinephrine is clinically acceptable for racepinephrine/epinephrine expectation.

label: X-Ray Computed Tomography
why: Generic CT is acceptable when the paragraph says computed tomography of head and no separate head-specific CUI is returned.

label: Potassium low
why: The DKA paragraph says low potassium; low potassium finding is clinically acceptable for the potassium target.

label: Electrocardiogram T wave
why: Electrocardiogram T-wave observable is acceptable for ECG wording in hyperkalemia context.

label: T Wave Peaked by ECG Finding
why: Peaked T waves by ECG is clinically acceptable for ECG wording in hyperkalemia context.

label: Sodium measurement
why: The hyponatremia paragraph says sodium was monitored; sodium measurement is acceptable for the sodium target.

label: Raised TSH level
why: Raised TSH level is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says elevated thyroid stimulating hormone.

label: Low free thyroxine
why: Low free thyroxine is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says low free thyroxine.

label: Anaphylactic shock
why: Anaphylactic shock is clinically acceptable for anaphylaxis in an acute reaction paragraph.

label: Cellulitis of leg
why: Cellulitis of leg is more specific and acceptable for a lower-leg cellulitis paragraph.

label: Cellulitis of lower leg
why: Cellulitis of lower leg is more specific and acceptable for a lower-leg cellulitis paragraph.

label: Drainage of abscess
why: Drainage of abscess is acceptable when the paragraph has a fluctuant abscess and incision/drainage.

label: Increased bilirubin level
why: Increased bilirubin level is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says elevated bilirubin.

label: Lipase
why: Base lipase concept is acceptable when the paragraph says elevated lipase.

label: Perianal fistula
why: Perianal fistula is more specific and acceptable for Crohn disease with perianal fistula.

label: Primary gout
why: Primary gout is clinically acceptable for gout flare wording.

label: Gout flare
why: Gout flare is clinically acceptable for gout flare wording.

label: Podagra
why: Podagra is clinically acceptable for gout affecting the first metatarsophalangeal joint.

label: History of fall
why: History of fall is acceptable when the hip-fracture paragraph says after a fall.

label: Fracture Fixation, Internal
why: Internal fracture fixation is clinically acceptable for ORIF wording.

label: Amnesia
why: Amnesia/memory loss is acceptable for progressive memory loss wording.

label: Resting Tremor
why: Resting tremor is more specific and acceptable for Parkinson tremor wording.

label: Cough with fever
why: Cough with fever is acceptable when influenza wording contains both cough and fever.

label: Cough with fever
why: Cough with fever is acceptable when influenza wording contains both cough and fever.

label: Vaginal Hemorrhage
why: Vaginal hemorrhage is clinically acceptable for vaginal bleeding wording.

label: Light Coagulation
why: Light coagulation is acceptable for laser photocoagulation wording.

why: Alternate optic nerve cupping CUI is acceptable for the same phrase.

label: Phacoemulsification of lens and insertion of intraocular lens
why: The combined cataract procedure is acceptable for phacoemulsification wording.

label: Bulging tympanic membrane
why: Bulging tympanic membrane is acceptable when the paragraph targets tympanic membrane findings.

label: Hyperkalemia (disorder)
why: Alternate hyperkalemia disorder CUI is acceptable for hyperkalemia wording.

label: Severe pain
why: Severe pain is acceptable for sickle-cell vaso-occlusive crisis pain wording.

label: Chronic diarrhea
why: Chronic diarrhea is acceptable when the paragraph says chronic diarrhea.

label: Uric acid level above reference range
why: Elevated uric acid is clinically acceptable for uric acid measurement wording in the gout paragraph.

label: Standard chest X-ray abnormal
why: Abnormal chest X-ray finding is clinically acceptable when the paragraph explicitly says abnormal chest X-ray.

label: Raised intraocular pressure
why: Raised intraocular pressure is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says elevated intraocular pressure.

label: Thyrotropin
why: The analyte thyrotropin is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says thyroid stimulating hormone and the measurement CUI is also retrieved.

label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
why: Alternate acute respiratory distress syndrome CUI is clinically acceptable for ARDS wording.

label: Kidney Failure, Acute
why: Acute kidney injury can resolve to the older UMLS acute kidney failure concept when that result carries acute kidney injury as a preferred or source label.

label: Transplant present
why: Transplant recipient/status is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says kidney transplant recipient and the procedure CUI is outside the first page.

label: Bacterial Endocarditis
why: Bacterial endocarditis is clinically acceptable when it is returned from the explicit infective endocarditis phrase.

label: Mitral valve vegetations
why: Specific mitral valve vegetation is clinically acceptable for a generic vegetation target in an endocarditis paragraph.

label: Double stranded anti DNA antibody
why: Antibody concept is clinically acceptable for double stranded DNA antibody wording when the assay CUI is lower ranked.

label: Pulmonary Surfactants
why: Pulmonary surfactants is clinically acceptable when neonatal respiratory distress wording says surfactant was given.

label: Oxygen saturation below reference range
why: Low oxygen saturation is clinically acceptable when neonatal respiratory distress wording says low oxygen saturation.

label: Fetal Macrosomia
why: Fetal macrosomia is the explicit condition named in the gestational diabetes paragraph.

label: buprenorphine / naloxone
why: The combination product is clinically acceptable when buprenorphine-naloxone is initiated.

label: buprenorphine / naloxone
why: The combination product is clinically acceptable when buprenorphine-naloxone is initiated.

label: Withdrawal Symptoms
why: Withdrawal symptoms is clinically acceptable for opioid withdrawal symptoms wording.

why: HLA-B27 antigen measurement is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says positive HLA-B27.

why: Specific ruptured abdominal aortic aneurysm satisfies the abdominal aortic aneurysm target when rupture is explicit.

label: Malignant neoplasm of lung
why: Lung malignancy is clinically acceptable for metastatic lung cancer wording when the result set also contains metastatic disease context.

label: Phosphorus
why: The analyte phosphorus is clinically acceptable when a nutrition/refeeding paragraph says phosphorus measurement.

label: CYP2C19 poor metabolizer
why: CYP2C19 poor metabolizer is clinically acceptable for CYP2C19 pharmacogenomic search wording.

label: CYP2C19 protein, human
why: CYP2C19 protein is clinically acceptable when the paragraph tests CYP2C19 pharmacogenomic retrieval and the gene CUI is lower ranked.

label: HIV Seropositivity
why: HIV seropositivity is clinically acceptable for an HIV patient pharmacogenomic screening paragraph.

label: Antimicrobial susceptibility
why: Antimicrobial susceptibility is clinically acceptable for antimicrobial susceptibility test wording.

label: Screening mammography
why: Screening mammography is clinically acceptable for mammography in preventive screening wording.

label: Human papillomavirus
why: The HPV organism concept is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says human papillomavirus test and screening-test context is explicit.

label: Urinary tract infection caused by Escherichia coli
why: Specific E. coli urinary tract infection is clinically acceptable for UTI in an ESBL E. coli UTI search.

label: Hemoglobin finding
why: Hemoglobin finding is clinically acceptable when a postpartum hemorrhage paragraph says hemoglobin dropped.

label: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease exacerbation
why: Specific COPD exacerbation CUI is clinically acceptable when chart-review wording says COPD exacerbation.

label: Severe hypoglycaemia
why: Spelling and source-specific CUI variant is clinically acceptable when the paragraph explicitly says severe hypoglycemia.

label: Urine output
why: Urine output is clinically acceptable for the urine volume target when the paragraph says urine output declined.

label: Croup
why: The standard croup CUI is clinically acceptable for the HPO/subglottic-laryngitis croup target.

label: Mini-mental state examination score
why: MMSE score is clinically acceptable when the paragraph explicitly discusses a declining Mini-Mental State Examination score.

label: Second degree burn injury
why: Second degree burn injury is the more specific burn concept when the paragraph says partial thickness second degree burn.

label: Exocrine pancreatic insufficiency
why: Exocrine pancreatic insufficiency is clinically acceptable for pancreatic insufficiency in cystic fibrosis.

label: Manic Episode
why: Manic episode is clinically acceptable when the paragraph says manic episode and mania persisted.

label: Measurement of lung diffusing capacity
why: Lung diffusing capacity measurement is clinically acceptable for reduced diffusing capacity in interstitial lung disease.

label: EGFR exon 19 deletion
why: EGFR exon 19 deletion is clinically acceptable for the EGFR target when oncology wording explicitly names that mutation.

why: CYP2C19 poor metabolizer is clinically acceptable for the poor-metabolizer pharmacogenomic result.
